---
title: "final_assignment"
format: html
---

For the course of the Meds Environmental Modeling class at UCSB, we have learned the step-by-step process of developing, testing, and analyzing results of a model for environmental problem solving contexts. In this project, we will develop a clear, well-structured plan for using a model in a hypothetical environmental context.

## Modeling Question

Inflammatory bowel disease and irritable bowel syndrome affect millions of people worldwide, impacting quality of life and health outcomes. There is research indicating a healthy gut microbiome can both decrease symptoms and perhaps decrease risk of developing either disease. We are interested in the impact of probiotic Kefir supplements on risk of inflammatory bowel disease and irritable bowel syndrome. Kefir increases the gut microbiome, which is thought to decrease disease risk. We will use a modeling framework to examine the relationship between a probiotic supplement and risk reduction by finding the optimal supplement intake for an increase in gastrointestinal track microbiome, and how much microbiome activity is necessary for disease risk reduction.

## Conceptual Model

## Model Design

Inputs:

Parameters:

Outputs:

Type of Model:

This hypothetical model was inspired by the work done by \_\_\_\_\_\_\_.

In order to test model performance, we will first test the model with a data set (source: \_\_\_\_) consistent of around 400 sample units with information of disease status and diet (fiber, protein, etc) over 26 years. We can input this data set (diet, specifically) into our model without including probiotics as a metric, and our output of disease status can be interpolated into risk and should look appropriate.

## Analysis Plan

## Assumptions
